title: Robust Smoky Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A citrusy and festive Tunisian dish featuring lamb, cranberries, and a blend of smoky spices, perfect for special occasions. This high-protein friendly tagine is slow-cooked to perfection in a rich and flavorful broth.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the dried cranberries, chicken broth, and browned lamb, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the tagine, and simmer the mixture for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the chopped parsley and lemon juice, and serve the tagine hot over couscous or rice.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
